task_completion.description: GeoGebra is open and an 'offset' slider is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra application window is visible. In the Algebra view (left panel) or Graphics view, there is a slider variable explicitly named 'offset' with a visible range (should be 0 to 30).

task_completion.description: Red light intervals are plotted for the 5 intersections over at least 120 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Graphics view, there are horizontal line segments, blocks, or points plotted at y-coordinates 0, 80, 160, 240, and 320. These segments should repeat periodically along the x-axis (representing time) up to at least x=120, showing the 30s red light durations.

task_completion.description: The car's trajectory is plotted as a line starting from the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagonal straight line is plotted starting exactly at the origin (0,0) and extending upwards and to the right. The Algebra view should show a line equation corresponding to the speed (e.g., y = 11.2x).

task_completion.description: The offset is adjusted so the car passes through the green waves.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The diagonal trajectory line successfully passes through the empty gaps (green lights) between the plotted red light intervals at all 5 intersection levels (y=0, 80, 160, 240, 320). The line must NOT intersect the red light segments.
The top title bar of the GeoGebra window displays 'green_wave.ggb' or 'green_wave', or a save dialog is visible showing this exact filename being saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the GeoGebra GUI (toolbars, algebra input, graphics view) rather than a terminal window running a Python script to generate the .ggb archive.

integrity.description: The diagram is built using mathematical objects, not an imported image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view on the left lists actual mathematical definitions (e.g., numbers, sequences, lines, functions, segments) rather than an imported picture/image object.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write the .ggb XML structure in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editors (like nano, vim, or VS Code) open showing raw XML or geogebra.xml files being manually edited to bypass the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a Space-Time Diagram in GeoGebra. The x-axis represents time (0 to 120s) and the y-axis represents distance. 5 intersections are located at y = 0, 80, 160, 240, and 320 meters. Red lights must be visualized as intervals (30s red, 30s green, 60s total cycle). A slider named 'offset' (0-30) must exist to shift the start of the green lights at each intersection. The car trajectory is a straight line starting at (0,0) with a slope of 11.2 (representing 11.2 m/s). The offset must be visually adjusted so the trajectory avoids all red light intervals. The final file must be saved as 'green_wave.ggb'.
